identity.research_question: Deep learning computational pipeline utilizing a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and Vector Quantized VAE (VQ-VAE) model (Tissue Microenvironment Encoder - TMEncoder) to extract explainable latent embeddings directly from multi-channel multiplexed images (multiplex CycIF) without prior cell segmentati
identity.project_summary: Deep learning computational pipeline utilizing a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and Vector Quantized VAE (VQ-VAE) model (Tissue Microenvironment Encoder - TMEncoder) to extract explainable latent embeddings directly from multi-channel multiplexed images (multiplex CycIF) without prior cell segmentation. Reduces 30+ channel dimensions into under 10 dimensions representing tissue archetypes. Applied to unsupervised image profiling (spatial motifs and recurrent cellular neighborhood mapping) and automatic ROI selection for spatial transcriptomics (GeoMx/Xenium) using latent space embeddings, downstream classifiers, and Grad-CAM visualization. Merged as a core component of Project 12: SPACE.
identity.responsible: Ziqi Kang (Matias Aiskovich, Olavi Goussev, Angela Szabo, Fernando Perez)
